body.processed:
Every family should have an emergency plan in place before disaster strikes. This comprehensive guide walks you through creating a plan that will keep your family safe during any emergency.

What Your Plan Should Include

  • Communication plan: How your family will contact each other if separated
  • Evacuation routes: Primary and secondary routes from your home and workplace
  • Meeting points: Designated locations where family members will reunite
  • Emergency contacts: List of local emergency services, doctors, and out-of-area contacts
  • Pet plan: Arrangements for pets during evacuations

Emergency Kit Checklist

  • 72-hour supply of water (1 gallon per person per day)
  • Non-perishable food and manual can opener
  • First aid kit and prescription medications
  • Flashlight, batteries, and hand-crank radio
  • Important documents in a waterproof container

body.processed:
The Community Resilience Toolkit provides local leaders, neighborhood organizations, and community groups with the tools and frameworks needed to prepare for, respond to, and recover from disasters.

Toolkit Components

  • Community risk assessment template
  • Volunteer coordination guide
  • Emergency communication plan for neighborhoods
  • Resource inventory worksheet
  • Post-disaster recovery planning framework

Building a Resilient Community

Research shows that communities with strong social networks and pre-established plans recover faster from disasters. This toolkit helps you build those connections before they are needed.
body.summary: The Community Resilience Toolkit provides local leaders, neighborhood organizations, and community groups with the tools and frameworks needed to prepare for, respond to, and recover from disasters.To

body.processed:
This manual provides comprehensive training materials for volunteers who wish to serve on Global Relief Partners disaster response teams.

Training Modules

  • Module 1: Introduction to disaster response and humanitarian principles
  • Module 2: Shelter management and displaced population support
  • Module 3: Emergency first aid and psychological first aid
  • Module 4: Mass feeding and water distribution operations
  • Module 5: Damage assessment and reporting
  • Module 6: Safety and security in disaster zones

Certification

Volunteers who complete all training modules and pass the assessment exam receive Disaster Response Volunteer certification, valid for three years.
